intro:
How to use this course

This workbook takes you from zero to CEFR A1 in Central Catalan, with one explicit goal: passing the official A1 exam (the Certificat de nivell inicial de català). Every unit builds towards the four exam skills — listening, reading, writing and speaking — and the book ends with a full mock exam, model answers and examiner tips.

The rhythm of a unit

One unit = one focused session (60–90 min). Read the theory, learn the New vocabulary (every unit adds 20–25 new words and 5–8 new phrases that are never repeated, so your stock grows to 300+ by the end), work the dialogue aloud, then do all the exercises in writing before checking the answer key. Each unit ends with a Resources box: one verified listening link and one online practice link.

Pronunciation: this book uses the IPA

Every Catalan word, phrase, example and dialogue line in this book carries a transcription in the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA), between slashes, e.g. llibre /ˈʎiβɾə/. The IPA is the international standard for writing sounds; learn the symbols once (next section) and you can pronounce any word in this book — and in any dictionary — without guesswork. The stress mark ˈ goes before the stressed syllable.

Finding & using the links

How to use the links • Every link is clickable in a PDF reader, and the address is printed so it works on paper too.
• On a LingoHut lesson page, the audio word-list comes with activities — look for Listening game, Matching game, Flashcards, Dictation — and click one to practise.
• No account needed for LingoHut, Loecsen, Intercat, verbs.cat, Talkpal or Forvo. Parla.cat (A2+) asks for free registration.
• If a link stops working, open the site's index below and search the topic title shown next to the link — titles stay stable even when addresses change.
• Index pages: LingoHut → lingohut.com/en/l114/learn-catalan · Loecsen → loecsen.com/en/learn-catalan · Easy Catalan (subtitled videos) → youtube.com/c/EasyCatalan · Intercat resources → intercat.cat/en/resources · verbs.cat → verbs.cat · Talkpal → talkpal.ai/catalan-grammar-exercises · Generalitat → llengua.gencat.cat
ipaGuide:
Reading the IPA — your pronunciation key

These are all the IPA symbols used in this book, with the closest English approximation. Central Catalan has a small, regular sound system: master this page and the transcriptions do the rest.

Vowels

IPASounds likeExample
a"a" in father (short)casa /ˈkazə/ — first a
ə"a" in sofa (the schwa — very common!)casa /ˈkazə/ — second a
ɛ"e" in bed (open)set /ˈsɛt/
e"ay" in say, but short, no glidemés /ˈmes/
i"ee" in seesis /ˈsis/
ɔ"o" in British hot (open)bo /ˈbɔ/
o"o" in note, short, no glidetot /ˈtot/
u"oo" in foodtu /ˈtu/
w, jglides: "w" in cow /aw/, "y" in boy /ɔj/nou /ˈnɔw/, mai /ˈmaj/
The golden rule of Central Catalan: unstressed a and e are pronounced /ə/, and unstressed o is pronounced /u/. That's why Barcelona is /bəɾsəˈlonə/ and color is /kuˈlo/.

Consonants that differ from English

IPASounds likeExample
ʎ"lli" in million (spelt ll)lluny /ˈʎuɲ/
ɲ"ni" in onion (spelt ny)any /ˈaɲ/
ʃ"sh" in shop (spelt x)peix /ˈpeʃ/
ʒ"s" in measure (spelt j, g+e/i)jo /ˈʒɔ/
tʃ"ch" in church (spelt tx, final -ig)cotxe /ˈkɔtʃə/, maig /ˈmatʃ/
dʒ"j" in jam (spelt tj/tg)formatge /fuɾˈmadʒə/
βa soft "b" with lips barely touchingavi /ˈaβi/
ð"th" in this, softcada /ˈkaðə/
ɣa soft "g", throat relaxedamiga /əˈmiɣə/
ɾsingle tap, like American "tt" in buttercara /ˈkaɾə/
rrolled/trilled r (rr, or word-initial r)carrer /kəˈre/
ŋ"ng" in singcinc /ˈsiŋk/
ˈstress mark — the NEXT syllable is stressedcatalà /kətəˈla/

Three habits worth knowing: written v sounds like b (/b/ or /β/); b/d/g between vowels soften to /β ð ɣ/; and the final -r of infinitives is silent: parlar /pəɾˈla/, dormir /duɾˈmi/.

examInfo:
Exam preparation

The Official A1 Exam

What the certificate is and who runs it

The qualification this course targets is the Certificat de nivell inicial de català (A1), aligned with the CEFR. Where you sit it depends on where you live:

In Catalonia

• The CPNL (Consorci per a la Normalització Lingüística) runs free Inicial (A1) courses for adults that lead to the certificate, all over Catalonia.
• University language services certify A1 through common interuniversity exams coordinated by CIFALC — open to the public, not only students.
→ Course & certificate routes, official portal: llengua.gencat.cat/en/serveis/aprendre_catala/a-catalunya
→ How the certificate levels work (A1 → C2): intercat.cat/en/certificates

Outside the Catalan-speaking area

The Institut Ramon Llull (IRL) runs the official exams abroad (150+ host sites, typically each May; registration opens in late winter). Be aware: the IRL ladder starts at Bàsic (A2) — there is no separate IRL A1 paper. So if you live abroad, this course is either (a) your preparation for an A1 sitting at a university CIFALC centre, or (b) the first, solid half of the road to the IRL A2 Bàsic, whose format the mock exam below mirrors at A1 difficulty.
→ What the IRL exams are: llull.cat/english/aprendre_catala/certificats_intro.cfm
→ How registration works: llull.cat/english/aprendre_catala/certificats_inscripcio.cfm

The four papers

A1/A2-level Catalan certificates test four skills. Weighting varies slightly by convener, but the shape is stable:

PaperWhat happensTypical length
1. Comprensió oral (listening)Short dialogues/announcements, played twice; tick true/false or multiple choice~15–20 min
2. Comprensió lectora (reading)Notices, short e-mails, simple texts; matching and multiple choice~30 min
3. Expressió escrita (writing)Fill in a form with personal data + write a short text (note/postcard, ±40 words)~30 min
4. Expressió oral (speaking)Personal questions (name, origin, family, routine, likes) + a simple role-play~10 min

What examiners reward at A1 — and the classic English-speaker traps

Reward: communication over perfection. Full short sentences (Em dic… / Soc de… / M'agrada…); correct ser/tenir; numbers and times said confidently; the vaig + infinitive past; asking for repetition in Catalan rather than freezing (Pots repetir, si us plau? earns goodwill — silence doesn't).

Traps: ① *Tinc trenta — age needs anys: Tinc trenta anys. ② *Soc un professor — no article with jobs: Soc professor. ③ agreement — *M'agraden el cafè (singular → agrada). ④ pronouncing every letter — final infinitive -r and final -t after n/l are silent. ⑤ answering Com estàs? with *Soc bé — it's Estic bé / Molt bé. ⑥ forgetting possessive articles — la meva germana, never *meva germana.

checklist.0: I can read Catalan aloud from spelling, and read IPA transcriptions.
checklist.1: I can greet, thank, apologise and ask someone to repeat or slow down.
checklist.2: I can introduce myself (name, spelling it aloud, origin, nationality, job, age).
checklist.3: I can use ser, estar, tenir and hi ha correctly.
checklist.4: I can count to 100, say prices, ages and phone numbers.
checklist.5: I can make articles, nouns and adjectives agree.
checklist.6: I can use the present tense of regular verbs and anar/fer/voler/poder.
checklist.7: I can describe my family and home with possessives and colours.
checklist.8: I can tell the time and talk about my daily routine with reflexive verbs.
checklist.9: I can order food, ask prices and answer with the pronoun en.
checklist.10: I can ask for and give directions.
checklist.11: I can express likes and dislikes with agradar and describe weather and seasons.
checklist.12: I can narrate the recent past with vaig + infinitive.
checklist.13: I can fill in a personal-data form and write a 40-word postcard.
checklist.14: I have sat the mock exam and reached the marking-guide threshold.

u1.block.4: A written accent marks the stressed syllable: grave è/ò = open /ɛ ɔ/ (cafè /kəˈfɛ/ coffee), acute é/ó = closed /e o/ (camió /kəmiˈo/ lorry). No accent? Words ending in a vowel, -s or -en are stressed on the second-to-last syllable; others on the last: hospital /uspiˈtal/ hospital, taula /ˈtawlə/ table.

u2.block.10:
Anna: Hola! Bon dia. Com et dius?

/ˈɔlə | ˌbɔn ˈdiə | ˌkom əd ˈdiws/ — Hi! Good morning. What's your name?

Tom: Em dic Tom. I tu?

/əm ˈdik ˈtɔm | i ˈtu/ — My name's Tom. And you?

Anna: Jo soc l'Anna. D'on ets?

/ˌʒɔ ˈsɔɡ ˈlannə | ˈdon ˈets/ — I'm Anna. Where are you from?

Tom: Soc de Manchester, soc anglès. I tu, ets catalana?

/ˌsɔɡ də ˈmantʃestəɾ | ˌsɔk əŋˈɡlɛs | i ˈtu | ˌedz kətəˈlanə/ — I'm from Manchester, I'm English. Are you Catalan?

Anna: Sí, soc de Girona. Molt de gust!

/ˈsi | ˌsɔɡ də ʒiˈɾonə | ˌmol də ˈɣust/ — Yes, I'm from Girona. Nice to meet you!

u5.block.12:
Laia: Què fas avui?

/ˌkɛ ˈfaz əˈβuj/ — What are you doing today?

Marc: Vaig a la feina, però a la tarda vull estudiar català.

/ˌbatʃ ə lə ˈfejnə | pəˌɾɔ ə lə ˌtaɾðə ˌβuʎ əstuðiˌa kətəˈla/ — I'm going to work, but in the afternoon I want to study Catalan.

Laia: Pots venir al cafè a les sis?

/ˌpɔdz bəˌni əl kəˌfɛ ə ləs ˈsis/ — Can you come to the café at six?

Marc: Sí, però no parlo gaire. Encara aprenc!

/ˈsi | pəˌɾɔ ˌno ˈpaɾlu ˈɣajɾə | əŋˌkaɾə əˈpɾɛŋ/ — Yes, but I don't speak much. I'm still learning!

u6.block.8:
— Tens germans?

/ˌtenz ʒəɾˈmans/ — Do you have siblings?

— Sí, tinc una germana. Es diu Clara i és metgessa.

/ˈsi | ˌtiŋk unə ʒəɾˈmanə | əz ˈðiw ˈklaɾə j ˌez məˈdʒɛsə/ — Yes, I have a sister. Her name is Clara and she's a doctor.

— Com és?

/ˌkom ˈes/ — What's she like?

— És alta, morena i molt intel·ligent.

/ˌez ˈaltə | muˈɾɛnə j ˌmol intəliˈʒen/ — She's tall, dark-haired and very intelligent.

u7.block.9:
— A quina hora et lleves?

/ə ˌkinə ˌɔɾə əd ˈʎeβəs/ — What time do you get up?

— Em llevo a les set i esmorzo a les vuit.

/əm ˌʎeβu ə ləs ˈsɛt i əzˌmoɾzu ə ləz ˈβujt/ — I get up at seven and have breakfast at eight.

— I què fas els dissabtes?

/i ˌkɛ ˈfaz əlz diˈsaptəs/ — And what do you do on Saturdays?

— Els dissabtes no treballo. Em llevo tard!

/əlz diˌsaptəz ˌno tɾəˈβaʎu | əm ˌʎeβu ˈtaɾt/ — On Saturdays I don't work. I get up late!

u8.title: Food, Shopping & the Pronoun en
u8.block.0: By the end you can: order in a café, shop, ask prices and use en — the role-play scenario most used in A1 oral exams.
u8.block.2: En /ən/ replaces an indefinite or quantified noun ("some / of it / of them"):
u8.block.3:
QuestionAnswer with en
Vols pa? /ˌbɔls ˈpa/ Want bread?Sí, en vull. /ˌsi əm ˈbuʎ/ Yes, I want some.
Quantes pomes vols? How many apples?En vull tres. /əm ˌbuʎ ˈtɾɛs/ I want three.
Tens cafè? Got coffee?No, no en tinc. /ˌno ˌno ən ˈtiŋk/ No, I don't have any.
u8.block.5: un quilo de /uŋ ˌkilu ðə/ a kilo of · mig quilo /ˌmitʃ ˈkilu/ half a kilo · una mica de /unə ˌmikə ðə/ a bit of. Price: Quant és? / Quant val? /ˌkwan ˈes/ /ˌkwam ˈbal/ How much is it?

Què voleu (prendre)?/ˌkɛ βuˈlɛw ˈpɾɛndɾə/What would you like (to have)?

u8.block.10:
Cambrer: Bon dia, què voleu?

/ˌbɔn ˈdiə | ˌkɛ βuˈlɛw/ — Good morning, what would you like?

Client: Un cafè amb llet i un entrepà de formatge, si us plau.

/uŋ kəˌfɛ əm ˈʎet i un əntɾəˌpa ðə fuɾˈmadʒə | si us ˈplaw/ — A coffee with milk and a cheese sandwich, please.

Cambrer: Molt bé. Alguna cosa més?

/ˌmol ˈbe | əlˌɣunə ˌkɔzə ˈmes/ — Very good. Anything else?

Client: No, gràcies. Quant és?

/ˈno ˈɡɾasiəs | ˌkwan ˈes/ — No, thanks. How much is it?

Cambrer: Són quatre euros amb cinquanta.

/ˌsoŋ ˌkwatɾə ˌɛwɾuz əm siŋˈkwantə/ — That's four euros fifty.

u9.block.0: By the end you can: ask for and give directions and locate things — common in both listening and oral papers.
u9.block.2: hi ha for existence (indefinite): A la plaça hi ha un mercat. /ə lə ˌplasə i ˈa um məɾˈkat/ There's a market in the square. ser/estar for locating a known thing: El mercat és a la plaça. /əl məɾˌkat ez ə lə ˈplasə/ The market is in the square.

Turista: Perdoni, on és l'estació?

/pəɾˈðoni | ˌon ez ləstəsiˈo/ — Excuse me, where is the station?

Local: Tot recte i després la primera a la dreta. És al costat del banc.

/ˌtot ˈrektə i ðəsˌpɾez lə pɾiˌmeɾə ə lə ˈðɾɛtə | ˌez əl kusˌtad dəl ˈbaŋ/ — Straight on, then first right. It's next to the bank.

Turista: És lluny?

/ˌez ˈʎuɲ/ — Is it far?

Local: No, és a prop. Cinc minuts.

/ˈno | ˌez ə ˈpɾɔp | ˌsiŋ miˈnuts/ — No, it's near. Five minutes.

u10.title: Likes & Free Time
u10.block.0: By the end you can: say what you like and dislike, talk about hobbies, seasons and weather. Exam skill: "M'agrada…" sentences are expected in both the writing and oral papers.
u10.block.2: It works "backwards": the thing liked is the grammatical subject. Use the indirect pronouns em, et, li, ens, us, els before it.
u10.block.3:
CatalanIPAEnglish
M'agrada el cafè./məˈɣɾaðə əl kəˈfɛ/I like coffee. (singular thing → agrada)
M'agraden els llibres./məˈɣɾaðən əls ˈʎiβɾəs/I like books. (plural thing → agraden)
T'agrada llegir?/təˈɣɾaðə ʎəˈʒi/Do you like reading? (verb → agrada)
Li agrada la música./li əˈɣɾaðə lə ˈmuzikə/He/She likes music.
No ens agrada gens./ˌnɔ ənz əˈɣɾaðə ˈʒens/We don't like it at all.
M'encanta!/məŋˈkantə/I love it!
u10.block.5: Catalan "does" the weather: Quin temps fa? /ˌkin ˈtems ˈfa/ What's the weather like? — Fa sol / fred / calor / vent. /fə ˈsɔl ˈfɾɛt kəˈlo ˈben/ It's sunny / cold / hot / windy. But: Plou. /ˈplɔw/ It's raining. · Neva. /ˈnɛβə/ It's snowing. · Està ennuvolat. /əsˈta ənnuβuˈlat/ It's cloudy.

Phrases

Quin temps fa avui?/ˌkin ˈtems ˈfa əˈβuj/What's the weather like today?

u10.block.8:
Laia: Què t'agrada fer el cap de setmana?

/ˌkɛ təˈɣɾaðə ˈfe əl ˌkab də səmˈmanə/ — What do you like doing at the weekend?

Marc: M'agrada llegir i córrer. I a tu?

/məˈɣɾaðə ʎəˈʒi i ˈkorə | i ə ˈtu/ — I like reading and running. And you?

Laia: A mi m'encanta cuinar. Però no m'agraden els esports.

/ə ˈmi məŋˈkantə kujˈna | pəˈɾɔ ˌnɔ məˈɣɾaðən əlz əsˈpɔrts/ — I love cooking. But I don't like sports.

Marc: Quin temps fa? Podem passejar?

/ˌkin ˈtems ˈfa | puˈðɛm pəsəˈʒa/ — What's the weather like? Can we go for a stroll?

Laia: Fa sol, però fa una mica de fred.

/fə ˈsɔl | pəˈɾɔ fə ˌunə ˌmikə ðə ˈfɾɛt/ — It's sunny, but a bit cold.

u11.title: The Recent Past
u11.block.0: By the end you can: say what you did, using the easiest past tense in Europe. Exam skill: the writing paper's postcard task almost always needs the past tense.
u11.block.2: Spoken Catalan's main past tense: special forms of anar + the plain infinitive. It works for every verb — only the auxiliary changes.
u11.block.3:
PersonAuxiliaryIPAExample
jovaig/ˈbatʃ/vaig parlar /ˌbatʃ pərˈla/ I spoke
tuvas/ˈbas/vas menjar /ˌbaz mənˈʒa/ you ate
ell/ellava/ˈba/va anar /ˌba əˈna/ he/she went
nosaltresvam/ˈbam/vam fer /ˌbam ˈfe/ we did/made
vosaltresvau/ˈbaw/vau veure /ˌbaw ˈβɛwɾə/ you (pl.) saw
ells/ellesvan/ˈban/van comprar /ˌbaŋ kumˈpɾa/ they bought
u11.block.4: Yes — "go" makes the past here, the mirror image of English "going to". Vaig anar /ˌbadʒ əˈna/ literally "I go to go" = I went.

Phrases
Què vas fer ahir?/ˌkɛ βas ˈfeɾ əˈi/What did you do yesterday?
Vaig anar a la platja./ˌbadʒ əˈnaɾ ə lə ˈpladʒə/I went to the beach.
On vas anar de vacances?/ˌon bəz əˈna ðə βəˈkansəs/Where did you go on holiday?
Va ser fantàstic!/ˌba ˈse fənˈtastik/It was fantastic!
M'ho vaig passar molt bé./ˌmu βatʃ pəˈsa ˌmol ˈbe/I had a great time.
Vam sopar fora./ˌbam suˈpa ˈfɔɾə/We ate out (dinner).
u11.block.7:
Nil: Què vas fer ahir?

/ˌkɛ βas ˈfeɾ əˈi/ — What did you do yesterday?

Júlia: Vaig anar al mercat i vaig comprar fruita. I tu?

/ˌbadʒ əˈnaɾ əl mərˈkat i ˌbatʃ kumˈpɾa ˈfɾujtə | i ˈtu/ — I went to the market and bought fruit. And you?

Nil: Vaig treballar tot el dia, però al vespre vam sopar fora.

/ˌbatʃ tɾəβəˈʎa ˌtot əl ˈdiə | pəˈɾɔ əl ˈβɛspɾə ˌbam suˈpa ˈfɔɾə/ — I worked all day, but in the evening we ate out.

Júlia: On vau anar?

/ˌom baw əˈna/ — Where did you go?

Nil: A un restaurant del barri. Va ser fantàstic!

/ə un rəstəwˈɾan dəl ˈβari | ˌba ˈse fənˈtastik/ — To a neighbourhood restaurant. It was fantastic!

u12.title: Consolidation & Exam Language
u12.block.0: By the end you can: combine Units 1–11 in connected Catalan, fill in forms, and use the language of the exam room itself.
u12.block.1: Reading (exam-style: no IPA, just like the real paper)
u12.block.2:
Em dic Sara i soc de Tarragona, però ara visc a Barcelona. Tinc vint-i-vuit anys i soc professora d'anglès. De dilluns a divendres em llevo a les set, esmorzo un cafè amb llet i vaig a la feina amb metro. M'agrada molt la meva feina. Els caps de setmana no treballo: m'agrada cuinar, llegir i passejar per la ciutat. Ahir vaig anar al mercat i vaig comprar fruita i peix. A la tarda va fer molt sol i vam passejar per la platja. M'encanta Barcelona!

My name is Sara and I'm from Tarragona, but now I live in Barcelona. I'm twenty-eight and I'm an English teacher. From Monday to Friday I get up at seven, have a white coffee for breakfast and go to work by metro. I really like my job. At weekends I don't work: I like cooking, reading and strolling around the city. Yesterday I went to the market and bought fruit and fish. In the afternoon it was very sunny and we walked along the beach. I love Barcelona!

mock.introNote: Sit this in one session of about 90 minutes, after Unit 12. Exam texts carry no IPA — just like the real papers. Answers and model texts are in the answer key.
mock.p2notice: BIBLIOTECA MUNICIPAL — Horari: de dilluns a divendres, de 9 h a 20 h. Dissabtes, de 10 h a 14 h. Diumenges: tancat. Per fer-se el carnet, cal un document d'identitat i una fotografia. El carnet és gratuït.
mock.p2aKeyHtml: 1) El diumenge. 2) A les deu (del matí). 3) Un document d'identitat i una fotografia. 4) Res — és gratuït.
mock.p3bTask: Task B — Write a postcard (35–45 words) to a Catalan friend from your holidays. Say: where you are, what the weather is like, two things you did yesterday (past tense!), and one thing you like about the place. Open with Hola, [name]! and close with Una abraçada, + your name.
mock.p3bModel: «Hola, Marta! Soc a Mallorca, de vacances. Fa molt sol i molta calor. Ahir vaig nedar al mar i vam sopar en un restaurant petit. M'agrada molt la platja d'aquí. Fins aviat! Una abraçada, Sam.»
mock.p4role: Part 2 — Role-play (the examiner plays the other part): You are at a café. Greet the waiter, order a drink and something to eat, ask how much it is, pay, thank them and say goodbye. Then, on the street, ask a stranger the way to the station and react to the answer.
mock.p4mark: Self-marking guide: give yourself one point per question for (a) a full sentence, (b) correct verb form, (c) clear pronunciation of /ə/, /ʎ/, /ʃ/. 18+ / 24 in Part 1 plus a complete role-play = comfortably at A1 standard.
mock.p4model: Em dic… / S'escriu… (spell it with the letter names from Unit 1) · Soc de… i visc a… · Tinc … anys · Soc … (no article!) · Sí, tinc un germà; és alt i simpàtic · M'agrada llegir i passejar · Em llevo a les set · Ahir vaig treballar i al vespre vaig sopar amb amics.
